title: Metal Detector Studio — telemetry grammar
description: Device-AGNOSTIC packet grammar for the bench lab. It defines packet types and generic field shapes only; concrete devices (number of harmonics, phase-diff names, raw ADC params, synth model) live in profiles/*.json. A generic mapper routes packets using these descriptors, so adding a new detector firmware means adding a profile, not rewriting the PC software.

transport.comment: One JSON object per line over USB-CDC / WebSocket. 'type' selects the packet; an active profile (see profiles/*.json) gives the concrete field layout.

hello.description: Sent once on connect. Binds the PC to the active device profile.

feature.description: Per-harmonic feature frame driven by the profile. Harmonics and phase diffs are keyed maps, so single-freq (1 harmonic) and multi-freq (N harmonics) devices share one packet shape.

harmonics.description: keyed by profile harmonic id (e.g. f1, f2, f3)

phase_diffs.description: keyed by profile phase-diff name (e.g. dphase31, dphase51); empty for single-freq devices

extras.description: optional scalars declared by the profile (e.g. gb_residual, temp_c)

raw.description: Raw RX ADC block for the virtual scope and live FFT. 'samples' length == profile.raw.block_size.

raw_iq.description: Raw demodulated I/Q block (e.g. TAKTYK ~1 kHz). For devices that demodulate in analog and expose no high-rate RX waveform; feeds the scope + baseband FFT. i/q same length.

config.description: Configuration command back to the device (and to the synthetic source). Allowed keys come from the active profile.
